threat_model: Evidence request and submission surfaces could collapse into authority if queued slots, submitted refs, or validators treat missing evidence as satisfied, submitted refs as accepted, or read-model projections as execution, connector, mutation, promotion, or terminal closure authority. The mitigation is schema-backed hard false authority fields, Unknown/AwaitingEvidence proof states, source drift rejection, focused negative tests, and preflight receipt order validation.

required_checks.check: request queue rejects execution, evidence acceptance, authority grant, promotion, and terminal closure drift
submission intake rejects evidence acceptance, evidence rejection, authority grant, promotion, and terminal closure drift
unsafe bundle compiler and queue sources fail closed before projection
workspace preflight receipt schema and example preserve canonical check order including the two new validators

findings.mitigation: The queue and intake schemas, validators, and tests hard-deny evidence_accepted, authority_granted, promotion_approved, terminal_closure_allowed, can_execute, can_mutate, and can_call_connector.
Submitted evidence refs are recorded as observations only, remain submitted_not_verified, and require a separate governed verifier before acceptance or authority changes.

mitigations: keep both surfaces local, deterministic, read-only, and Foundation Mode scoped
fail closed on unsafe source authority drift
preserve Unknown/AwaitingEvidence until separate verifier evidence exists
bind validators into workspace governance preflight and receipt contract
record both surfaces in proof coverage as read_model audit_chain surfaces
